bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid. The opponent’s opening offer gives me only moderate utility, and with no messages or concession pattern yet, I will anchor on my strongest values across the high-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early (t=0.02) and the opponent has simply repeated their initial offer, which gives me only 0.442 utility. To avoid repeating my exact prior bid while making a low-cost concession, I concede on issueD to their proposed valueC, while holding firm on the three high-weight issues.

I am targeting maximum utility (1.00) because it is still very early at t=0.04 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the same low-utility offer for me. Since their behavior is firm and there are no messages indicating meaningful tradeoffs, I should hold my high-weight issues and avoid further concessions for now while not repeating my immediately previous bid.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still very early in the negotiation at t=0.06. The opponent has repeated the same valid offer several times and has not shown concession, so I will concede only on my lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the three high-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.08) and the opponent has repeated the same offer without conceding. Their offer gives me only moderate utility, so I should not move toward it yet; I will keep firm on my high-weight issues and avoid repeating my immediately previous bid.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making a small concession on my least important issue. The opponent has repeated the same offer without conceding, and their bid is only moderate for me, so I will not move on high-weight issues yet; I will vary from my last bid by offering their signaled issueD valueC.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only limited concessions. The opponent has repeated the same valid offer without conceding, so I will hold firm on my highest-weight priorities issueA and issueB, while varying the offer slightly to avoid simply repeating my last bid.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making only a small concession on the lowest-weight issueD. The opponent has repeated the exact same offer with no concession, so I should not move on my high-weight issues yet; issueD: valueC matches what they keep proposing and still gives me strong utility.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still early at t=0.16, and the opponent has not shown any concession, repeating the same offer each time. Their repeated offer gives me relatively low utility, so I will hold firm on my high-weight issues rather than reward their lack of movement.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.961, conceding only on the low-weight issueD while holding firm on the three high-weight issues. Since it is still early at t=0.18 and the opponent has repeated the same offer without conceding, there is little reason to make a larger concession yet, but issueD:valueC matches their repeated preference signal and may improve acceptability.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid rather than making major concessions. The opponent has repeated the same valid offer with no concession, which is worth only about 0.442 to me, so I will hold firm on the high-weight issues and vary only the low-weight issue compared with my previous bid.

At t=0.22 it is still early, so I am targeting a very high utility bid while making a small concession on the lowest-weight issueD. The opponent has repeated the same offer without concession, and their preferred issueD valueC is relatively cheap for me compared with conceding on issueA, issueB, or issueC.

At t=0.24 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for me while making a meaningful concession on issueD and issueB, which the opponent has consistently proposed. The opponent has held completely firm on the same valid offer, so I am not matching their low-utility bid, but I am testing whether conceding one high-weight issue partially can break the deadlock while preserving my top values on issueA and issueC.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.70 while making a modest additional concession because we are past the opening phase but not yet near the deadline. The opponent has repeated the same valid bid without conceding, so I am holding firm on high-value issueA while keeping concessions on issueB and issueD and slightly softening issueC from my ideal.

At t=0.28 it is still early-mid negotiation, so I am targeting a strong utility while making some concessions on issueB and issueD that the opponent has consistently signaled in their offers. The opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering the same bid worth only about 0.44 to me, so I will hold firm on high-importance issueA and keep issueC favorable rather than moving all the way to their position.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.811, making a moderate concession while it is still early-mid negotiation time. The opponent has held completely firm on the same bid, which is only about 0.442 utility for me, and there are no messages to change that read. I concede issueC and issueD to values they have repeatedly signaled, while holding issueA and issueB at my best values for now.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.77 while making a meaningful new concession, since we are in early mid-game rather than near the deadline. The opponent has held completely firm on the same valid bid, so I am probing whether conceding on issueA and the low-weight issueD while holding my best values on issueB and issueC can move them.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.77 while avoiding repeating my previous exact bid; at t=0.34 there is some pressure to explore tradeoffs, but not enough to collapse to the opponent’s fixed offer. The opponent has held completely firm on issueA=valueD, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueC, issueD=valueC, so I am conceding issueB and issueD while holding my highest-value issueA and issueC positions.

I am targeting a utility around 0.77: a meaningful concession from my maximum while still protecting my high-weight issues. At t=0.36 there is some pressure to move beyond my earlier hardline offers, but it is not late enough to accept the opponent’s unchanged low-utility bid. The opponent has repeatedly offered issueA=valueD and issueD=valueC, so I concede those while holding issueB and issueC closer to my interests.

I am targeting a moderate concession bid with utility about 0.637, still well above the opponent’s fixed offer value to me of about 0.442. Since time is only 0.38, there is pressure to move beyond my earlier high-utility bids, but not enough to accept their unchanged position; the opponent has shown no concession and no messages, so I concede on issueB, issueC, and issueD while holding my strongest value on issueA.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.616: still clearly above the opponent’s standing offer to me, but with meaningful concessions given we are at t=0.40. The opponent has held completely firm on issueA=valueD, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueC, issueD=valueC, so I am testing a stronger concession on their apparent priorities while holding issueB at my top value.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.59, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier high-utility bids while still well above the opponent’s repeated offer utility to me. The opponent has shown no concession at all, always repeating issueA=valueD, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueC, issueD=valueC, so I am offering them most of those signaled preferences while holding firm on issueC, one of my highest-weight issues.

The opponent has repeated the exact same bid with no concession, so I should not reward that too much yet at t=0.44. I am targeting a moderate-utility compromise above 0.6 by conceding on issueA, issueC, and issueD while holding firm on issueB, one of my high-weight issues, and avoiding repeating my immediately previous bid.

I targeted a mid-level utility around 0.51 by conceding on issueA, issueB, and issueD toward the opponent’s repeated position while keeping a partial gain on issueC. At t=0.46 there is some time pressure but not enough to simply copy their full standing offer, especially since they have shown no concession at all. Their repeated bids strongly signal they prefer issueA=valueD, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueC, issueD=valueC, so this offer moves close to that while still protecting some value for me.

At t=0.48, I am targeting a mid-utility compromise rather than my maximum bid, since the opponent has been very firm around issueB=valueB, issueC=valueC, and issueD=valueC, with only a recent movement on issueA. I am conceding on issueA, issueB, and issueD while holding issueC at my top value because issueC is highly important to me and the opponent has not shown real concession on it.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.59, conceding on issueB and issueD and using the opponent’s long-standing preference signal on issueA while holding firm on issueC, which is highly valuable to me. The opponent has mostly repeated the same bid and has not meaningfully improved my utility, so at t=0.50 I should make a plausible compromise without dropping to their exact low-utility offer. There are no opponent messages to weigh, so I rely only on the bid history.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.55, conceding on issueA, issueB, and issueD while holding issueC at my top value because issueC has high weight for me. The opponent has mostly repeated the same bid and their recent change on issueA actually lowers my utility, so I should not concede all the way to their standing offer yet. With no messages to interpret, I am using their bids as the signal and offering a compromise aligned with their apparent preferences except on issueC.

I targeted a moderate concession while still staying above the opponent’s repeated offer utility to me; their latest bid gives me only about 0.397, so I am not ready to match it at t=0.54. The opponent has mostly held firm around issueB=valueB, issueC=valueC, issueD=valueC and recently shifted issueA to valueC, so I am conceding on issueA/issueB/issueD while keeping issueC at valueA as a compromise instead of my top valueB.

At t=0.56 I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.55 rather than dropping to the opponent's standing offer, since their bids have not improved for me and actually moved slightly worse on issueA. The opponent consistently signals valueB on issueB, valueC on issueD, and likely valueC on issueA, so I concede those while holding issueC at my top valueB, one of my high-weight issues.

At t=0.58 I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.59 rather than dropping to the opponent’s low-utility bid, since there is still time left and the opponent has shown little real concession. Their latest offer indicates they may accept issueA=valueD and issueB=valueC, so I concede there and on low-weight issueD, while holding issueC at my best value.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.51 by conceding on issueA, issueB, and issueD while still protecting issueC, one of my high-weight issues, with a compromise value rather than dropping to the opponent’s latest low-utility bid. At t=0.60 there is meaningful time pressure, but the opponent’s offers have not improved for me and have even worsened recently, so I should concede further without fully accepting their standing position yet.

I am targeting a mid-level utility around 0.55 because we are past the midpoint but not yet at the deadline, so I should concede on lower/less promising issues while preserving value on the most important remaining tradeoff. The opponent has mostly held firm around issueB:valueB, issueC:valueC, issueD:valueC and recently shifted issueA to valueC, so I concede on issueA, issueB, and issueD while still asking for issueC:valueB, which is my strongest value on an important issue.

I am targeting a moderate utility offer around 0.44 because we are past the midpoint and the opponent has remained mostly firm rather than steadily improving their offers. Their repeated bids strongly signal they want issueA around valueD/valueC, issueB valueB, issueC valueC, and issueD valueC, with no natural-language messages to alter that read. I am therefore conceding on the opponent’s most consistent positions while keeping the bid above reservation value and using only valid domain values.
